overview: Hatch is the closest direct competitor -- they do AI text-back for home services. However, their AI uses template-based responses rather than truly intelligent conversation. Their opaque pricing and reported contract lock-ins are additional vulnerabilities.

weaknesses.weakness: Template-based AI responses that feel robotic and generic
weaknesses.talk_track: Hatch uses templates -- pre-written responses that try to match what the customer said. But home services customers have specific, nuanced questions. 'My AC is blowing warm air and making a clicking noise' deserves a smart response, not 'Thanks for reaching out! Let me connect you with our team.' NeverMiss actually understands the conversation.
weaknesses.evidence: User reviews consistently mention robotic or generic responses; demo recordings show template patterns

weaknesses.weakness: Opaque pricing requires sales call -- cannot self-serve
weaknesses.talk_track: Ever tried to find Hatch's pricing? You can't. They make you sit through a sales call to find out what it costs. At NeverMiss, it's $297/month. Right there. No games, no 'let me check with my manager,' no surprise contract terms.
weaknesses.evidence: No public pricing page; multiple forums discussing inability to get pricing without sales demo

weaknesses.weakness: Long-term contract lock-ins reported by users
weaknesses.talk_track: Hatch users on forums report being locked into annual contracts. If it's not working, you're stuck paying. NeverMiss is month-to-month. If we don't earn your business every month, you can leave. That's how confident we are.
weaknesses.evidence: Forum posts and review mentions of contract requirements

weaknesses.talk_track: Hatch uses the same template framework for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and roofing. A burst pipe at midnight needs a different response than a thermostat question. NeverMiss learns the specifics of YOUR business and YOUR services, not just your industry.
weaknesses.evidence: Product demos show generic industry templates rather than business-specific learning

weaknesses.weakness: Slow to adapt templates for new service categories or seasonal changes
weaknesses.talk_track: When you add a new service or the season changes, Hatch needs manual template updates. NeverMiss adapts automatically because it understands your business, not just keywords.
weaknesses.evidence: User reviews mention lag time for template updates when business offerings change

our_advantages.advantage: Truly intelligent AI vs template matching
our_advantages.proof_point: NeverMiss understands context and generates appropriate responses; does not match to pre-written templates
$297/mo published openly. No sales call required to learn the price.
AI learns specific services, service areas, pricing, and scheduling for each business
Add a service, change your hours -- NeverMiss adapts without manual template editing

talk_tracks.when_lead_mentions_hatch: Hatch is in the same space as us -- AI text-back for home services. The difference is how the AI works. Hatch matches your customer's message to a pre-written template. NeverMiss actually understands what they're saying and responds intelligently. Try asking both a curveball question and see which one sounds like a real person.
talk_tracks.when_lead_is_comparing_prices: Hatch won't even tell you the price without a sales call. That's usually a sign it's higher than they want you to compare. NeverMiss is $297/mo, right up front, month-to-month. No contracts, no hidden fees.
talk_tracks.when_lead_values_ai_texting: If you like the idea of AI text-back, you'll love NeverMiss. We're purpose-built for it. The key difference vs Hatch is intelligence -- our AI learns your specific business instead of matching templates. Homeowners can tell the difference.
talk_tracks.when_lead_is_current_hatch_customer: How are your response quality scores with Hatch? A lot of folks come to us because their customers complained about generic-sounding texts. NeverMiss responses read like a knowledgeable team member wrote them, not a bot. Happy to show you a side-by-side comparison.

deploy_when: Lead mentions template-based responses or generic AI
Lead asks about automated follow-up tools
CRM notes indicate current Hatch customer
Lead complains about robotic or generic automated responses

counter_objections.hatch_is_built_for_home_services: So are we. The difference is depth. Hatch uses industry-wide templates. NeverMiss learns YOUR specific business. Two HVAC companies in the same city have different services, pricing, and availability. NeverMiss handles that. Hatch sends them the same template.
counter_objections.hatch_has_integrations_with_my_crm: NeverMiss integrates with your workflow too. But integrations are table stakes -- what matters is what happens when the customer texts. Template response or intelligent conversation?
counter_objections.my_hatch_rep_showed_good_results: Demo responses are always the best-case scenario. Ask to see responses to unusual questions -- the ones that don't match a template. That's where the difference shows.
